body:
**💥 Breaking:** If `Field.Label` encloses `Switch`/`Checkbox`/`Radio`, the `htmlFor`/`id` attributes are no longer explicitly set to associate them

Demo: https://deploy-preview-2036--base-ui.netlify.app/experiments/form

This enables multiple `Field.Label`s in a single field for radio/checkbox groups. Implicit association of the label and label-able element is preferred by wrapping `Field.Label` around the component:
```jsx
<Field.Label>
  <Switch.Root />
</Field.Label>

<RadioGroup>
  <Field.Label>
    <Radio.Root />
  </Field.Label>
  <Field.Label>
    <Radio.Root />
  </Field.Label>
</RadioGroup>
```

For radio/checkbox group the implicit association works even when `nativeButton={false}`, so the `for`/`aria-labelledby` attributes don't need to be added back unless even the `label` is replaced with a `span`, in which case it should be on the user to ensure the labelling is correct.

Closes https://github.com/mui/base-ui/issues/1982
Closes https://github.com/mui/base-ui/issues/1308

- [x] I have followed (at least) the [PR section of the contributing guide](https://github.com/mui/base-ui/blob/HEAD/CONTRIBUTING.md#sending-a-pull-request).
title: [field][switch][checkbox][radio] Support implicit `Field.Label`
